titleStmt.title: Mr. Alexander G. Kirk, Political Adviser, Allied Force Headquarters, to the Secretary of State

head.hi: Mr. Alexander G. Kirk, Political Adviser, Allied Force Headquarters, to the Secretary of State

div.p:
1183. Reference my 1124, March 24, 4 p.m.Not printed. Stevenson, British Ambassador to Belgrade, has been informed by KardeljEdvard Kardelj, Yugoslav Second Vice Premier and Minister for the Constituent Assembly. and SubasicIvan Subasich, Yugoslav Minister for Foreign Affairs. that the Government of Jugoslavia considered Albania a sovereign and independent state. The Jugoslav Foreign Minister added that the Jugoslav Government did not contemplate federation
with Albania and the future would show whether it would be possible to organize a federation of Balkan peoples. He added that the Government of Jugoslavia did not intend to organize now any such federation.
Sent Department, repeated to Moscow as 97.
